channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Bitcoin is being replaced — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin is being replaced

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:49 UTC

OP

This is, with my whole hearted belief, the next Bitcoin.

Last year there were murmurs here of Bitcoin being set to be artificially under attack in 2026 in order to make way for a biometric UBI blockchain.

All wars are bankers wars.

Anon posted a website that since went down (kingtree.net) and had the archives scrubbed. On it was a forum from 2006 to 2009 that laid out the original code and implementation of Bitcoin. The code was, per the post and forum, stolen and commodified. Satoshi was there.

At the same time, the url ankh.cash was registered and dormant. Same name of the kingtree protocol (ANKH). The blockchain running since 2006 went offline, but is now live and open source on the ankh.cash url. It's been made public on February 28 2026 at 3:33am ET (numerology relevance) on Github.

I've monitored it since, and over the past two weeks, at the start of the Iran and Israel war, it began verifying biometrics and users onto the chain and distributing.

I downloaded a node and went through the biometric data. From my analysis and attempts at deciphering - it is being implemented in early stages in South America, South East Asia, and East Africa - the global south. Based on phenotypes of facial ratios.

The financial system is set to be imploded on purpose to make way for Ankh and its derivatives. Biometric blockchain based UBI for the masses - one code, one hash and tokenization of people's entire livelihood.

Early replies (first 3)

62008048
>>62008039
big if true

62008050
>>62008039
Ahhh man
*checks pants*
Yeah I've got some nasty poopie in there.
*Takes pants off and tips them upside down*
Ahhh man now it's all in your thread.

Financially speaking I this will decimate me and that's why this is on topic.

62008079
keep me posted

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62008083
>>62008039
>>62008048
>>62008050
>>62008079

samefag

62008088
>>62008083
fagsame

62008092
>>62008088
famesag

62008099
>>62008083
all me

62008108
>>62008092
sagfame

62008109
>>62008083
You're pointing and screaming into the void

62008323
>>62008039
When bitcoin came out, it wasn't the next anything, it was itself.

62008333
>>62008039
>capped at 9 billion
I thought it was 500m

62008341
>>62008039
Yeah say hello to those who understand face training, bone health, hormones and the light angles...woman can even play with makep and face fillers too...this cannot be just that simple

62008358
Ok so Whats the ticker ANKH is like $200 mcap cant buy it on SOL

62008512
>>62008358
>buy it
buy it? as a goy? ahahaha
you vill have to work for zem

62008699
theres literally no point to buying this because it literally says its to allocate 2.7 million ie 1 ankh per person via biometric identification.

Sorry but you have a critical flaw in your shillcoin, theres no reason to buy

62009087
>>62008333
0.0
Can't tell if blessed or cursed digits

62012161
I spent an entire day's worth of compute power decoding, deciphering and reconstructing the biometric data of the first person verified on this blockchain.

Node details:

"index": 159,
"timestamp": 1772259107285.0525,
"hash": "0xc697fe256c7ec05764ad5822dd845e41d0c91b7848c01d680f1327c98ecb88a1",

"validator": "system",
"stateRoot": "0x098d578a30f40e43f9348c5d63aef8cce289c28faabd7ca98f84c49c71167b6a",
"transactionsRoot": "0xe182ae9f084ec96a4a32f5693c97cc0643748cc4d33cd30ecd9bf3976c975c26",

"sidechainId": null,
"transactions": [
{
"id": "2646f826-a0e5-4208-96e5-aa9540cbcd9a",

"nonce": 0,
"data": {

"biometricTemplateHash": "932a461e62200978f15a8643ba0e4b06258864ca5d8e5558d1db03cdce2367dc"

62012192
Just tell me where to buy it fag

62012289
Another one. These reconstructions are approximations based on the biometric data. These few are likely individuals in the shadows. Looks a bit like Bill Ackman. Would like to see if anyone else can reconstruct this from the data and get the same image.

{
"id": "f8de47b6-d2f1-4021-a222-f46e0fd48a5f",
"type": "BIOMETRIC_REGISTRATION",
"from": "ankh_f585c14699e6a5aaac7ff3a0fdf8427dc88c6fab",
"to": "system",

"fee": "0",

"biometricHash": "762aa1edc2221af8ec73731e40e25747571acdc5e8f94e2f7bbc37b36dc68d8c",
"biometricTemplateHash": "762aa1edc2221af8ec73731e40e25747571acdc5e8f94e2f7bbc37b36dc68d8c"

62012688
>>62008323
And then came XMR.

62013151
MASS VERIFICATION EVENT DETECTED

Uptick in verifications. Investigating and reporting back with updates.

62013172
>>62008039
>This is, with my whole hearted belief, the next Bitcoin.
-t every shitcoin baggot ever

62013176
>>62012688
based

62013395
Confirmed implementation in the Horn of Africa - Bab Al-Mandab Strait. The mass verification event came from there.

What this means, and my analysis:

A sidechain built on the Ankh blockchain is being implemented in the north coast of Somalia with a separatist or sovereign movement verifying biometrics for the population in the region and issuing currency to them.

With the conflict in Iran and the Strait of Hormuz disruptions, somebody is playing their cards and placing their bets on gaining control over another strait in the region, potentially controlling the currency supply and asserting control over the trade routes.

I'm still digging, but this is very interesting and gives credence to my hypothesis. Much of this may have been planned in the last months of 2025. Biometric data elsewhere in the chain also points to Argentina being verified and active on the mainnet.

62013469
The .cash domain extension wasn't added until 2014

You are a faggot

62013472
>>62008039
>This new cryptocurrency will replace Bitcoin and will be equally distributed across every person in the world
>Anyways, here's where you can go buy as much as you want - before it moons!

62013631
>>62008039
>look guys i repackaged worldcoin. Give me your eyeball scans!

62013636
>>62008109
Leave the void-screamers alone

item.title: >LOAN FORGIVENESS FOR ME BUT NOT FOR YOU this generation will be the most broke in history. — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>LOAN FORGIVENESS FOR ME BUT NOT FOR YOU this generation will be the most broke in history.

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:49 UTC

OP

>LOAN FORGIVENESS FOR ME BUT NOT FOR YOU
this generation will be the most broke in history.
who will buy your stocks and crypto now?

Early replies (first 3)

62013196
loans wouldn't be a problem if interest was paid after the principle

literally all loan-related problems are from people upset they've payed $40k and only $4k of that went towards the principle

62013204
>>62013196
How about instead we tell the dumb niggers that get mad at this that the interest is paying astronauts to build a wall in space to protect them from aliens?

62013206
>>62013196
How about we just ban usury (again?)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62013208
>>62013206
if you can convince the libertarians that a 40% interest rate isn't doing anyone any favors then you have my support

62013229
>>62013196
Would that look so much better?

>I paid 200k of my 250k loan but still have to pay 200k in interest after the principal

62013237
>>62012736
the high IQ move is to get on an income driven repayment scheme (RAP or IBR) and choose to manually re-certify your income (i.e., don't use your most recent tax forms) with minimum wage photoshopped pay stubs.

>t-that's illegal!!! you'll get caught!!!!

62013328
>>62012736
>Nooo I took out a loan and signed my name on the contract and everything why am I being expected to honor the contract that's not faaaaaiiiiir
You did it to yourselves. Now grow the fuck up and pay it back.

62013334
>>62013237
The high IQ move would have been to convince the university to give you enough scholarships and fellowships that there was nothing left for you pay.

62013341
>>62013334
it's state dependent. i graduated with a state university (in pennsylvania) with a 3.8 GPA in applied math. never once received a dime of scholarship aid.

62013359
>>62013196
Huh? Extra payments each month should be applied to your principal amount. Mathematically, paying an extra couple hundred here and there actually make a big difference over the life of a loan. As far as total interest goes, anyway.
If your bank is not applying extra money towards principal, you're getting super-jewed. Like, that's a whole other level of usury lol.

62013368
>>62013341
>it's state dependent.
Nope. It's very IQ dependent though. You get offered those scholarships and fellowships in high school. By the time you actually show up to university it's too late, you're already enrolled and paying your own way and they have nothing to gain by throwing money at you. They're an enticement for high IQ prospects, not a reward for studying hard.

62013372
>>62012736
Car salesman here

It won’t matter. Retards will always find a way.
You can try to talk them down to more reasonable purchase (doesn’t matter to me, in fact the cheaper the vehicle usually the better the profit excepting the absolute max platinum super high rollers package).

They won’t hear of it.
They want their AC seats and they will go 20% interest rolling 15k to do it, and they will do it with you or find someone else.

62013377
>>62013359
He's saying he wants all of each payment to go towards the principle. Which he can totally do, but that has to be written into the loan agreement. It's his failure, but that won't stop him from whining about how "unfair" it is that the contract he read and signed is now suddenly not to his particular preferences, rather than simply paying down the principle at an accelerated rate by paying more than the absolute minimum each month Because whining is free and easy and doesn't involve difficult things like adulting.

62013510
>>62013377
Yeah let me just call up the Department of Education and negotiate a unique loan agreement. Some people don't have any good options. Doesn't mean we should let international bankers prey on them.

62013544
>>62013368
no, it's literally dependent on how your state funds public schools. florida, for example, has a very generous and easy to get "bright stars" scholarship. pennsylvania has no such program and comparatively expensive state schools, because they chose to underfund colleges.

what's the deal with kinky haired muttalo zoomers running off their big ugly gorilla lips when they haven't the slightest idea?

62013583
>>62013196
This. Usury has killed mankind. We live in a parasite state and world

62013593
>>62013196
Loans wouldn't be a problem if people didn't take out loans with larger monthly payments than they could afford to pay.
The problem is not the system, the problem is people's financial literacy.
Or lack thereof.

62013624
>>62013593
>Loans wouldn't be a problem if people didn't take out loans with larger monthly payments than they could afford to pay.

this argument is meaningless when you look at how quickly certain fields of study can be "disrupted." software development was almost a sure bet if you weren't retarded. within 5 years it was destroyed by a combination of outsourced indians, AI, corporations funding bootcamps, and the media attracting students. similar things have happened in law, accounting, and pharmacy. similar things are in the works for certain areas of medicine and dentistry.

62013630
>>62013206
Economy slows down without usury.
>heh GOOD
Yeah except all other countries won't be slowed down and your retarded ass country will be lapped and left behind in no time. And then probably raped and conquered and erased from history. It's a doggy dog world

62013635
>>62012736
i don't understand the op pic. is there a point in this screenshot? explain please

item.title: Why don't more retail traders try to learn the techniques of quantitative finance? — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why don't more retail traders try to learn the techniques of quantitative finance?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:52 UTC

OP

Why don't more retail traders try to learn the techniques of quantitative finance? I'm trying to do it myself, and outside of the difficulty of getting practical experience and actually applying the theory, the theory itself is not that hard. I think it's because retail traders overall are just really dumb people, but there are still many who have the capacity to make it work.

The math is accessible to anyone with an engineering, math, or physics degree. Basically it's mostly linear algebra and statistics. Those companies are hiring people with just bachelors degrees in CS for quant positions will tell you that. Yes they are hiring the best new grads with 4.0 GPAs from MIT but they still don't somehow just know PhD level math. Stochastic calculus is a meme from 20 years ago when all quants did was work on the sell side pricing exotic derivatives for banks using models with stochastic differential equations. You don't need it and it's not even really helpful, and there are rigorous books on that deal with all the mathematical prerequisites anyways if you really wanted to learn it.

It's not impossible and you don't need to be a super genius to use quant strategies to benefit your retail trading. You just need to be a decent programmer and have decent applied math knowledge. The entire mystique around quant is just gatekeeping trying to keep you from making money so that only hedge funds can use these strategies and models, because they don't want retail investors to make it. They don't want you to be armed with the tools they have so you can keep being exit liquidity.

Early replies (first 3)

62010454
>it's not fucking rocket science, but none of you would know about rocket science because you're all fucking retards probably

62010482
Thanks Anon

62010520
>Why isn't anyone doing what PHD level mathematicians backed by billions of dollars of capital are doing? The math is right in front of you!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62010545
I'm gonna calculate the hecking bivariate gaussian distribution of the implied vomma aaargghghhhhfg fuck I just shitted myself

62010567
>>62010187
the market isn't normally distributed. that doesn't mean to use laplace, that means to look for the finger under the cloth stepping outside of brownian motion. thanks for reading my ted talk.

62010960
>>62010187
I find gemini 3.1 pro is good enough to check the math if you frame the problem correctly, and it's fairly easy to learn the theory from books.
The problem it's like competing with poos for twitter impressions. It's a penny for million business. The big firms are doing it on a large scale and no commisions, and can scrap some profit. A single investor will hardly break even after commisions.

You can have some luck selling options, if you can price them correctly to sell insurance for a small premium.
But that fucks with your risk profile, so might as well go 2x SPY and hope for the best.

There is very little free money on the market, you have to pick bets and take risk if you want to outperform an ETF.

62010972
>>62010960
>perform like ETF
quiet boomer

62010988
>>62010187
because a true artist can click buy and sell based on feelings and outperform any automated math goy autist hft thing
it's true but you will convince yourself u need to learn high level calculus or something
youve already convinced yourself of this, havent you?

62011150
>>62010567
I just wanted a pic of a monte carlo simulation

62011368
>>62010187
AI can give you a roadmap to self-learning this stuff.
Lots of free courses online for mathematics, along with problem sheets (also get AI to make those).
Even ask the AI to give you a pretest before you learn a new concept and take that test again when you finish the concept to measure progress.
WAGMI. Ignorance is NOT strength.

62011434
>>62010187
Because its garbage hidden under a pile of math. Huge waste of time and mental capacity

62011437
>>62010545
Kek

62011438
>>62010187
Because I'm not smart enough and I know I'm not smart enough

62011449
I'm about to take a brownian motion in my toilet if you kno wwhat I mean

62011453
Also if you're TRADING rather than INVESTING then as a retail trader swinging letfs you should only use less than 10k or whatever so you don't rape yourself into oblivion.

62013568
>>62010988
you can't do HFT as a retail investor anyway

62013638
You cannot have the red without the blue
It's all zero sum

62013645
>>62013638
i'll have red, and leave the blue to you. simple

item.title: Can someone give me examples of people who became their own boss, or started a business that became successful over time… — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Can someone give me examples of people who became their own boss, or started a business that became successful over time…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:49 UTC

OP

Can someone give me examples of people who became their own boss, or started a business that became successful over time, by using LLMs (a.k.a., """AI""")?

No, I don't mean selling """AI""" or """AI"""-related stuff, I mean building actual useful & successful products or services.

Also, what happened to all the AI-blockchain buzzword bullshit? LMAO

Pic unrelated.

Early replies (first 3)

62012353
>>62012349
Type some shit into claude? it automatically does it

consider if you have a university .edu email you can combine it with free gemini pro prompts

62012361
>>62012349
I am my own boss and have a successful business that was built on the back of AI (first ChatGPT, now Claude).
You just need a good idea and some basic knowledge of how computers work (Linux experience is a plus).
No I will not tell you what my business is or what we do.

62012394
>>62012353
Did you even try reading what I wrote?

>>62012361
>anonymous 4chan user says he's an example
definition of example, from google:

/Jɡˈzɑːmpl,ɛɡˈzɑːmpl/
1.a thing characteristic of its kind or illustrating a general rule.

2. a person or thing regarded in terms of their fitness to be imitated.
"it is important that parents should set an example"

3. verb
be illustrated or exemplified.

some anonymous post can NOT be an example, RETARD.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62012578
>>62012349
Chat bots cannot make you rich except by conning and tricking others. Chat bots do not do work, and the only value added to civilization that justifies money being exchanged or created is is through actual work. Chat bots don't do work, so they can only make you rich through scams.

62012629
>>62012353
Isn't it funny that ANY student can access this for free? Almost like they're intentionally dumbing down the population, but they would never!

62013502
>>62012629
I'm ok with this, as long as the retarded techbros that push for this crap manage to bankrupt themselves in the process

62013551
>>62012349
there are plenty, but they are ai-subscription based and hardly get use, if your product is using the ai-cloud, it's no different subscription-slop than signing up for premium gpt or claude, it's just tailored to a specific use case which a smart person would figure their money would be better spent on the all-around model and so it's a race to the bottom with subscription products with tailored niche use cases
local ads and car ads are filled with ai-gen slop commercials, but that is not what you are asking about

62013622
>>62012629
I wouldn't blame AI for that. AI is great for both helping you learn and helping you cheat. The fact that everyone chooses to use it for cheating isn't AI's fault

62013634
I automated half my department with these and got promoted to Assistant Director from a regular wagie, my career is now on a meteoric rise and our VP is aware of my existence

You're either gonna be like the wagies I got fired or you can be me but imagine not taking advantage from literally the most disruptive technology ever

item.title: Anyone want unlimited online courses (1000+) for cheaper? Found a workaround — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone want unlimited online courses (1000+) for cheaper? Found a workaround

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:40 UTC

OP

Hey everyone,

I’m aware of this site called UniversalClass and thought I’d share because it’s actually pretty solid if you’re into learning new skills, or skills that lead into job opportunities.

They have a Platinum Membership that gives you access to 1000+ online courses (business, finance, IT, writing, personal development, etc.), all self-paced with certificates included.

Normally it’s around $189/year or $29/month after the first month, which isn’t terrible, but I know how to get a platinum membership for far cheaper

Here’s the link: https://www.universalclass.com/platinum-membership.htm

Why it might be worth it:
• Unlimited access to all courses (not pay-per-course)
• Self-paced (start/stop anytime)
• Certificates + CEUs for completed courses
• Works on phone/PC so you can do it whenever


If you’re someone who likes platforms like Udemy/Coursera but hate paying per course, this is kinda like a Netflix version of learning.

Not trying to spam just thought some people here might find it useful

If anyone’s used it before, keen to hear your thoughts. If you’re interested please do not hesitate to contact me.

Telegram-@onlydoffyy
Discord- onlydoffyy

Early replies (first 3)

62013460
>>62013454
I know how to get this cheaper.
>But I'm not going to tell you unless you join my telegram/discord.

Ok buddy

62013463
Indians like you need to be strung up and beaten and if you keep pushing your luck it'll happen.

62013483
Brother I’m not Indian I have full proof I can assure you I’m fully legit the reason I know about this loophole is because I’m in a place which provides these certificates and courses to help get my visa back

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62013489
Join my telegram or discord? These are my usernames to my account so we can speak privately if you are interested not some scam lmao

62013509
>>62013489
You just say it here then. There's like 30-50 people here who comment.

62013554
>>62013483
>Brother I’m not Indian I have full proof I can assure you I’m fully legit the reason I know about this loophole is because I’m in a place which provides these certificates and courses to help get my visa back
Yeah, whatever, fecal golem. Sage.

62013620
This is how it works you add your details in here once done then you’ll have access to all courses. It’s pretty valuable if you’re trying to obtain skills for a job or career you’re interested in I have done this many times for many people

item.title: Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake — 54 replies — 69.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:06 UTC

OP

I’ve been telling folks here not to sell BTC and now they’re buying at the top.

>When are you gonna learn your lesson, folks? HODL

Early replies (first 3)

61996853
>>61996755
rich kid but black

61996883
>>61996853
What's the problem?

61998635
>>61996755
Sell and wait for 10k per btc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003136
>>62003088
It's 0.07% APR. And you will be sending your BTC to an anonymous system whose owner pinky promises to send you your money back if you ask nicely.

62003422
>>62003088
It’s possible, just look into it yourself. you’ll get it. Self custody is the advantage.

62003430
>>61996755
Let the goyim deal in the crypto world.

62003444
>>62003136
“Anonymous system” lol. It’s literally built around cryptographic guarantees, not trust. APR is low, sure, but better than nothing. This isn’t sending BTC to some random yield farm.

62003448
>>61998652
He's a great kiddo

62003660
>>62003430
Bitcoin keeps improving. Lightning, Taproot, and other upgrades make BTC faster, cheaper, and more usable, confidence grows, price follows.

62003668
Market sentiment drives crypto hard. A little media buzz, some whales buying, and suddenly BTC is pumping. FOMO is real.

62003679
>>62003444
The only defi right now is ethereum

62003695
>>62003117
Whats your price target?

62003910
>>62003679
“Only Ethereum” is outdated. Solana and Sui already have real DeFi, and BTC is just getting started. Most of it is still sleeping, not because it can’t be used.

62003914
>>62003111
Smart guy for buying the dip

62004044
>>61996755
I am blaming Trump for cryoto dumps

62004059
your president is greedy ass

62004175
>>61996755
>Buy XRP and LINK instead

62004400
>>61996755
I need you guys to pump my vaporware up over $96,000 so that I can make my exit. you guys know how this works, so don't play dumb. your exit will come at some point in the future, trust the plan.

62005462
>>62003660
I guess you know better than I do.

62006331
>>62003668
You mean manipulation?

62006772
>>62004400
buy memecoins and enjoy

62007988
>>62004044
You mean the ongoing war conflict? I didn't sign up for that

62008412
>>62003910
How are we making money there?

62008424
>>61996755
BUY ICP

62008434
>>62008412
it's risky. Don't ever try

62010261
>>62008412
You can stake your BTC there natively. If you want more, better wait for their upcoming Trustless Bitcoin Vaults, they’ll open use cases like lending and more.
>>62008434
Risky how? You even know what you’re talking about?

62010279
>>62006772
Which memecoins? Lol

62010288
>>62004400
Then trust your plan ser

62010298
>>62008424
This coin is not really giving me profits

62010313
I've been DCAing since we dumped

62010369
That's right saar do not redeem the BTC 100k again very soon

62012799
>>62010313
That's nice ser

62013543
>>62010369
More than 100k saar

item.title: > buy wallet > cable is USB C to C > none of my devices support USB C Do they expect me to be rich already? — 21 replies — 22.1/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-03-21 14:55 UTC

OP

> buy wallet
> cable is USB C to C
> none of my devices support USB C
Do they expect me to be rich already?

Early replies (first 3)

62007422
>>62007396
You can buy USBC to USBA adapters off Amazon for around a dollar.

62007436
>>62007396
>none of my devices support USB C
Frugal king, I bow

62007454
>>62007396
Your phone is micro USB?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007467
>>62007396
Thats a dongle not a wallet

62007859
>>62007396
thank fuck i bought one and locked my btc at 126k

62007860
If you were really poor you would know you don't need a glorified USB stick to make a wallet.

62007877
>>62007396
my 10 year old laptop has usbc....

62007912
>>62007877
My thinkpad don't

62007927
>>62007912
my thinkpad t480 from 2017, which is the one i was referring to, does

62008132
>>62007396
Make an electrum or wasabi wallet. Even phone wallets like trust wallet are safe if you are vigilant and not keeping more than 10k usd in them

62008135
>>62007422
I'm too poor for that.

62008138
>>62008135
kys

62008142
>>62008138
I will keep living, just for you.

62008466
>>62007927

62008470
>>62007396

Also
>trezor

62008488

Get with the times, grandpa.

62008546
>>62008135

62008585

My ledger nano S cacle is broken. I buy a new micro usb to usb a cable. IT DOESNT FIT
FFS WHY KI TOLD ME I NEED THAT

62008851

62012431
>>62007396
Phones come with an adapter...

62013521
>>62007396
You now have a perfect excuse to purchase a used thinkpad with a USB C adapter, reformat it and install Linux, and have a Banking / Finance/ Crypto only computer with good opsec.

item.title: USA is removing sanctions on Iranian oil — 43 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
USA is removing sanctions on Iranian oil

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-21 13:14 UTC

OP

This is going to crash the oil market!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62005086
>>62005061
based bussyent

62005090
>>62005061
stupid, they should be forced to sell it to us for $$30 a barrel.

62005101
The message is that they fucked it up so hard they're begging the enemy they want to destroy to sell them OIL lmfao. Sure, faggot, what about a tariff for Amerimutts plus you pay in yuan, sodomite.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62006750
>>62005061
Lmao what a fucking joke. Desperation.

62008663
>>62005061
Is this a humiliation ritual? If they were going to embarrass themselves like this they should never have started the war to begin with. What a fuckup.

62008682
>>62005234
Its not like Iran couldnt make use of the payments.

62008783
>>62006729
Sounds accurate

62008815
>>62008663
>Is this a humiliation ritual? If they were going to embarrass themselves like this
this is the trump presidency in a nutshell.

62008854
>>62008682
The idea behind avoiding destruction of their oil is to leave them something to surrender to. If that was gone they would truly be on deaths ground with nothing left to lose. By leaving them something to lose, you increase leverage for a negotiated settlement. Without the oil they have 0 reason to negotiate, with there may be a situation where they say fuck this let's focus on the oil gains

62009078
>>62006698
>>62006729
>>62008854
3 out of 20
15% non indian posts
Grim

62012144
>>62005453
American geopolitics is mostly two thing.
1/ Destroy europa, and prevent the Berlin-Moscow axis. The more europa sink, the more america take over it's markets.
2/ Maintain the petrodollar, and crush anyone who dare sell it's oil not in dollars (Saddam Hussein, Gaddafhi, Maduro (end 2025), Iran...).

62012155
Bro, let us buy your oil so that we can bomb you, bro. We're totally winning, I'm tired of winning, bro, why are you bombing our bases, bro. Please, stop, we're winning, bro.

62012261
>>62005453
>>62012144
Believe it or not...no one really cares about Europe. Europe is a bunch of tourist destinations that play no significant role in the global economy.

What is the biggest European economy? Germany? And they've been nose diving without imports from other nations like Russia and without as big a Chinese market.

62012273
>>62012261
you would have no industry if it wasn't for all the highly technical products you buy from germany, france and netherlands

62012285
>>62005246
We already have venezuela's oil under American custodianship that is also severely hindering the world's trade as well. This is just a 2 week break to let the dust settle before the Russian and Chinese frog is boiled in even hotter water. If Iranian regime change is effectively successful, the United States literally controls the majority of trade. This also has massive implications on the reunification of Taiwan because if the Changs can't fuel their war machine, theyre SOL.

ALTHOUGH what im saying is purely speculative, but if Iran is beaten down enough and the IRGC is out of power, the Ukraine war is also over, due to attrition.

If Russia and China can't get illegal Iranian or Venezuelan oil. It's game over.

62012292
>>62012285
>We already have venezuela's oil under American custodianship that is also severely hindering the world's trade as well
ahahahahahaha. yeah, venezuela will magically fix their non-working oil machinery, re-import their lost expertise, and the shitty venezuelan oil will somehow be refined and distributed in 2 more weeks.

62012296
>>62012292
Thats also a valid assertion given that theyre ran by communists monkeys.

62012444
>>62005061
Trump is the greatest strategist of all time
and by strategist I mean the highest ranked dementia patient of all time

62012471
>>62005061
they're getting desperate

62012484
>>62005061
Wasn't China buying their oil anyway, so this would just mean China could just flip the Iran oil for a profit, what does this do

62012488
>>62012471
>A is the X; B is the Y
Slop detected + opinion discarded

62012490
>>62012471
>gold price will go up because mentally ill, everything-controlling lolbertarian techbros and shit
imagine believing this bullshit lmao

62012498
>>62005206
>what happens
Nukes if they keep going I imagine.

62012504
This reeks of sheer desperation.

62012787
>>62012471
This is some grade-A quality schizo theory and I love it.

62012801
>>62005061
this is meaningless rhetoric and I'm tired of this blatantly bullshit market manipulation every fucking week
the oil was only sanctioned to NATOcucks and Iran got their money in Chinese bank accounts which orange niggers couldn't touch even if he rimmed Netanyahu on an emergency broadcast
no NATOcuck country is going to touch Iranian oil and it will still flow to China so what he's saying is essentially "uhh don't worry guys we won't blow their oil up we know how fucked up things are" which is like NO FUCKING SHIT
I'm so fucking tired of this retarded nigger administration

62013223
>>62005061
Hahahaha these fucking clowns

62013268
I still think nothing ever happens and it'll be okay but Iran and their backers have the once in a generation opportunity to really kick america HARD in the balls

It remains to be seen whether they will want to take it

62013281
>>62005061
it happened yesterday OFAC lifted the restriction through an announcment on their site

62013287
>>62012498
That's not going to bring the oil back

62013304
>>62005061
They are just scared of China involving itself in the war using Iran as a proxy.

62013322
>>62005061
hmmm
maybe they're preparing for taking that island and using that oil

62013373
>>62005061
The goals of the war in iran:
1) Help russia by making oil expensive
2) hurt europe and usa

item.title: They flipped the switch — 47 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
They flipped the switch

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-03-21 12:56 UTC

OP

Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point. Well, here it is. The silent flip. Chainlink has permeated the gates of Washington and has considerable influence at this point that was built carefully and mostly out of the spotlight, because regulatory ambiguity around digital assets made overt positioning impossible.

Understand that in groups where powerful men meet and make critical decisions, Chainlink is being positioned as connective infrastructure for the next generation of finance, state-level coordination, and machine-verifiable systems. Monetary policy rails, yes we all know that. But also cyber defense architecture, government identity-adjacent workflows, and potentially even admin systems where the ability to be audited lives in the public forum.

The recent announcement from the SEC/CFTC that deems Chainlink as a commodity is the green light. I can tell you that Economics 2.0 will be announced shortly and the slides are literally being prepared as we speak. The next major milestone after that is staking expansion this summer.

There is still a question about how much friction individual states can still create on the margins (CA, NJ, SC, WI) but the decision makers don't care about that. Hope you don't live in any such places. Most people will not understand what they are looking at until after the repricing. That is how these transitions always work. By the time the headlines catch up, the asymmetry is gone.

Early replies (first 3)

62007047
WW3 is starting and you're still shilling about this gay, irrelevant scamcoin.

62007066
>>62007042
You’re going to have to get a job chief. Typing out your delusions on an indoor kite flying forum isn’t going to pay the bills. Remember, eye contact and firm handshake

62007080
Where's insider anon's larp again?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007902
3.3 mill inflow on ETFS today.

62007905
>>62007902
the most of ANY crypto ETF today btw

62007938
so when do i sell and at what price

62007973
>>62007905
holy samefag

62008011
>>62007902
ur mom had 3.3 mill inflow today. of semen.
#rekt

62008018
>>62007387
>long-term stakers a bone?
eat shit buddy. Many of us can't even get in the pool. Be happy you've gotten the 4.5% and some random memecoins.

62008356
>>62008018
congratulations on being a latefag. you'll have to make do with sdl

62008363
>>62007042
Finally

My ducks are in a row

62008438
The copium is real in this thread. It's inevitable that we will transition to digital currency and AI oversight. You know it. I know it. It's just a matter of when. And I believe OP's prediction is a rational prediction.

62008468
>$2 above where it was when I first started researching crypto
I figured back then it'd be something I rotated gains into as it seemed to have long-term potential and it seems that's panning out better than I thought.

62008498
>>62007042
>jew id

Holy shit I think this may be a sign

62008529
>>62008498
It's a sign alright

62008569
>>62007042
artemis you suck at larping
you have no panache, no flair for drama
you have no personality

62008595
what the make it by next year stack?

62010029
>>62008569
yeah instead he talks like someone who touches grass (unlike you) and insight (also unlike you), which makes him believable. he's called things right and will be right again dumb fuck

62010118
>>62010029
>omg he's the real insider
i swear atleast one of you fags pop up for every larp. i too knew something was coming, but i kept it to myself because i hate you normal fags and your egos.

62010146
>>62007042
>She still thinks Rome happened on an investment forum
Ya'll deserve to lose money if you dont understand recent history

62010190
>>62010029
he hasn't called shit, shut the fuck up

62012054
>>62010190
>>62010118
check the archive you dummies, oh and touch grass

62012321
>>62012054
It's all fake. Proof is the chart, if any of it were true you would see being priced in.

62012392
>>62012054
>touch grass
you don't even know what your saying newfag

62012758
Price. Chart.
Adem. Won
He won. Linkies lost.

62012774
>>62012321
The longer it moves in lock step with trash like Cardano, the more I doubt.

62012804
>>62007042
> Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point.
thats me. I knew it was march. Its always march. Obvious really.

62012857
>>62012804
Lmaoo it was always X date guys trust le plan

62013253
>>62007042
Right on cue, the next Plato is here to bore us to death on his "theories" and Roman shit

62013315
>>62007042
Artemis, feed us more hopium.

62013349
>>62010118
>>62010029
yeah I've been in every thread telling him he's bad at this.

Cool he gave lukewarm predictions (nothing actionable for the reader) that came true. Now people know that Artemis has some amount of insider information. We don't know his motive, we don't have any reason to believe he wouldn't tell 3 useless truths and then lead everyone off a cliff with an actionable lie.

Artemis actively rejected putting any SOUL into his larp. He wouldn't post a picture of the DC skyline. He didn't post a random trinket like a bar napkin to prove his association with people who make decisions. He is stingy with personal information... cowardly even.

He put nothing on the line. No hints of personality, no connection. Nobody trusts him, nobody cares what he has to say except in hindsight. I think he's either a low level narcissistic staffer who just wants "in on the story of chainlink" for bragging rights. Or he is a sanctioned insider on a mission to achieve some reaction from retail holders. But we don't care what you have to say if you dont play the game.

62013350
>>62013349
Why do you people come here just to LLM post? What's the point nigger?

item.title: Dollar = Doge — 13 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Dollar = Doge

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-21 14:28 UTC

OP

I know, I know, Doge is a memcoin, but at this point so is the dollar. It's just a meme that's been around longer and has a stronger reputation. Well Dogecoin is steadily gaining a similar status whether you see it or not. If you could imagine it, why wouldn't Doge become the new Dollar. I mean it already shares the first two letters as the "Do"llar. It's got a strong community backing. The Goverment brought in a legitmate DOGE department. Elon Musk has already used it as a sort of gift shop trial run for Tesla. He even recently dropped another meme AI video of him as the Dogefather. Doge-1 space mission might be launching this year. DOGE was one of the 16 cryptos that made it to the list for Clarity or potential Clarity, as commodity. X payments going live in April, with rumors saying 4/20 Dogecoin implementation or maybe news update on its integration.

I'm sure there is even more things I'm missing, but bottomline, who truly knows the future of Crypto and which ones will take over. Dogecoin seems like it may be ramping up for something much bigger than others have precieved.

Early replies (first 3)

62009117
>>62009107
Doge will be the #2 coin soon. Doge is the new ETH killer.

62009134
>>62009107
Its unironically better money than USD
That's not a high bar, fiat is just such shit
What happened to all the crypto payment processor protocols (some advertising doge) that were supposed to be rolling out last year?
Its like the whole bullrun narrative (I.e. actually doing shit with the technologies we have available) hit the pause button because of political bullshit

62009147
>>62009117
That's a huge stretch, but it's in the number 9 slot on coinmarketcap and X payment integration could 100% skyrocket it. I hope so.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62009167
>>62009134
Fiat is shit, but was good when backed by gold. I agree it would be nice to see society using technology to its full potential, but I still think technology for the masses is a little too much to handle, especially Boomers, and in general money related technology needs lot of R&D testing to prove out the bugs.

62009223
>>62009147
It would only have to be $1.50~ to overtake ETH. And once you realize that Doge could literally triple in price 0.30 in a day or two from those big red short candles, 1.50 becomes really possible.

62009247
>>62009223
WoW, suppose you're right. I didn't think it was that close in comparison to ETH. This makes my thread post even more relevant to the potential of Dogecoin. Thanks for your response. This just re-enforces Dogecoin more.

62009312
>>62009107
DOGE is the silver to BTC gold.

62009330
>>62009312
I'm down with that! (^o^)

62009592
>>62009247
I believe in doge
Not because its DA BESTEST but because its ascent would be such a slap in the face to srsbzns projects that all got coopted and nerfed into the ground (like btc to an extent); mass adoption would be the funniest outcome, and therefore fitting for fake and gay clownworld

62010053
>>62009592
It really would be hilarious to see Doge or even Shib come out on top and be known as the common new Dollar and then all the people who missed the boat on it kicking themselves for passing up something so stupid. With how the world is and how silly it's become, Dogecoin as a US currency doesn't seem to far fetched.

62011089
>>62009117
CHECKED

62012826
>>62009107
They say that every doge has his day.

62013474
>>62012826
CHECKED

>Oh that's during my working hours, I guess I can't book an appointment with you

WHAT THE FUCK DO CONSUMERS EXPECT? AM I CRAZY OR ARE CONSUMER RETARDED?

62009844
Take a sickie nigga, I make 1500 a day and I took the day off so I can stay at home jerk off and play video games lol

62009942
>>62009844
I was just looking at a conversation between a lead and an AI agent I created.

62013412
>>62009942
you want their money. they told you what's stopping them from giving you their money. your reaction is reeing. that's why you are poor

62013429
>>62009824
I always wondered what would happen if you ran a business that operates from 6pm to 4am. Like you wouldn't have any competitors. You can probably charge them a premium and people will probably pay it. I like working at night anyways and you wouldn't have to put any sunscreen on to go to work.

62013430

item.title: The play for the weekend: -Short crypto. — 7 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The play for the weekend: -Short crypto.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-21 14:32 UTC

OP

The play for the weekend:

-Short crypto. BTC and eth are good options for shorting right now
-Long BRENTOIL and WTIOIL
-Close saturday/sunday (depending on your time zone, take some profits on saturday if you decide to close on sunday, sometimes the sunday pump gets frontrun)

That's it. You still have some time. Trump's temper tantrum don't start until American markets close. You're welcome.

Early replies (first 3)

62010299
Such a youthful young boy.

62010723
>>62010270
I'm 38 and I look like him

62013295
>>62010270
I am 38 and I look younger

>>62010270

>>62010270
